title: A two-row row-stochastic operator preserves constant vectors

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If each row of a two-by-two operator has weights summing to one, then the operator preserves constant two-vectors.

declarations.name: row2Apply
declarations.statement: def row2Apply (a b x y : Real) : Real := a * x + b * y

declarations.name: rowStochastic2First
declarations.statement: def rowStochastic2First (a b x y : Real) : Real := row2Apply a b x y

declarations.name: rowStochastic2Second
declarations.statement: def rowStochastic2Second (c d x y : Real) : Real := row2Apply c d x y

declarations.name: rowStochastic2_const
declarations.statement: theorem rowStochastic2_const (a b c d u : Real) (hrow1 : a + b = 1) (hrow2 : c + d = 1) : rowStochastic2First a b u u = u /\ rowStochastic2Second c d u u = u

tasks.input: Translate constant-vector preservation for a two-row row-stochastic operator into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem rowStochastic2_const (a b c d u : Real) (hrow1 : a + b = 1) (hrow2 : c + d = 1) : rowStochastic2First a b u u = u /\ rowStochastic2Second c d u u = u

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by applying row2Apply_const to each row.
tasks.target: Split the conjunction and solve each row with row2Apply_const.

tasks.input: Find the LeanNumerics lemma needed for each coordinate of row-stochastic constant-vector preservation.
